# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v2
      with:
        node-version: '14'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm install

    - name: Run custom build commands
      run: |
        if [ -n "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}" ]; then
          echo "Running custom build commands:"
          echo "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}"
          eval ${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}
        else
          echo "No custom build commands provided. Running default build."
          npm run build
        fi
      env:
        BUILD_COMMANDS: ${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}

    - name: Upload build artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
      with:
        name: build-artifacts
        path: dist/
